title: A Very Old Handprint in a Cave
summary: Scientists found a handprint on a cave wall in Indonesia that is 67,800 years old, making it the oldest art ever discovered. This handprint was made by people who lived long ago, and it helps us learn about how humans first traveled to Australia. The handprint is in a limestone cave on the island of Sulawesi, and researchers used a special dating method to figure out its age. They also found that people kept making art in this cave for thousands of years. The handprint looks a bit like a claw because someone changed its shape after it was made, but we don't know why. This discovery shows that early humans were creative and had a rich culture.

background_read: This article talks about a discovery in Indonesia where scientists found a very old handprint in a cave. It helps us understand how early humans lived and moved to new places like Australia. The handprint is special because it is the oldest art we know, showing that people have been creative for a long time. Scientists used tools to date it and learned that art was made in the cave for many years.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from universities and research agencies
WHAT: Found a 67,800-year-old handprint in a cave
WHERE: In Indonesia, on the island of Sulawesi
WHY: To learn about early human art and migration

perspectives.description: The handprint shows early human creativity and helps trace migration.
